title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Go Build Room
summary: A public-interest newsroom incrementally builds idempotent versioned entry ingestion model from original RSS, Atom, HTTP, and PostgreSQL fixtures while retaining prior Go, HTTP-client, HTTP-server, SQL, Git, accessibility, security, testing, cancellation, and recovery requirements. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of idempotent versioned entry ingestion model while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Entry Key Scope
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop Go team handles case 6655 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Entry Key Scope.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis. For Feed aggregation and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Feed Go Entry Key Scope, keep Feed Go Entry Key Scope connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
